feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Fiori
feed.subtitle: SAP Fiori Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Classical Workflow release task for PR PO not working form My Inbox
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have migrated from ECC to S4HANA 2023. Now client wants to use mobile for releasing the task for PO and PR.  My Inbox is ready with but issue is Release is not working from My Inbox but working fine when done from backend.

when clicking on claim and then release the mesasge is recieved on screen that task is released but neither it diappears from my inbox, not its actually released. 

There is no decision option maintained, client wants no modifications on workflow.

the question is Why the standard is not working, My problem is same as asked by Anand in below Q&A: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/my-inbox-app-issue-in-release-task/qaq-p/11547578

Please provide the Valuable inputs.

Regards

Rakesh

entry.title: Disable tabs in "My Bank Data (S/4HANA)" F3112A
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I can't seem to find the setting where you could disable certain tabs within the app "My Bank Data (S/4HANA)" F3112A. As far as I know this could be done within the table V_T7XSSPERSUBTY for the older V2 for the app F3112.

Do any of you know whether this can be set for the newer version in S/4HANA?

 

Thank you in advance and kind regards

entry.title: ECP Custom Development
entry.content:
Hi,

We are about to start with the process of moving our on-prem ECC system to SAP SuccessFactors EC/ECP. We have been using SAP HR in ECC for about 24 years so as a big company we have lots of custom developments in the payroll space. I am familiar with SuccessFactors most of these custom developments wont be catered for in SuccessFactors. I am curious as to what type of development is allowed in ECP as its running on S/4 and what other companies have done with their custom infotypes and other WebDynpro and Fiori applications? How much custom development can you do on ECP? Did you move your Fiori applications to CAP or RAP applications? Are custom infotypes still allowed? I assume you can build ODATA services? Can you build custom programs for instance if you have a custom posting report, can you use that development on ECP as well? 

Kind Regards

Deon

entry.title: SAP Fiori My Travel and Expenses V2
entry.content:
Hello, experts!

 

We are facing an error on My Travel and Expenses V2. When opening selection screen where are many reports and we try to use scroling for searching the report there are duplicated these ones. 

Do you have an idea to solve this problem please?

Best regards,

Hanna

entry.title: Project Cost Line Items – Loss of Cumulative and Commitment-Based Measures in Recent Releases
entry.content:
Project Cost Line Items – Loss of Cumulative and Commitment-Based Measures in Recent Releases

I would like to raise a concern regarding the evolution of the Project Cost Line Items application.

In older S/4HANA releases, the application F2538 – Project Cost Line Items provided several analytical measures that were extremely useful for project controlling and cost analysis.

Reference:

  • SAP Help document 

  • SAP Fiori App Library: F2538

As shown in SAP documentation and screenshots from earlier releases, users could analyze project costs using measures such as:

  • Cumulative Actual Cost

  • Cumulative Planned Cost

  • Cumulative Actual Cost + Commitments

  • Commitment values originating from open Purchase Requisitions and Purchase Orders

These measures enabled the creation of trend analyses and S-curves, allowing project managers to compare:

  • Planned vs. Actual evolution over time

  • Actual + Commitment evolution over time

  • Cost performance by fiscal period and fiscal year using cumulative values

The chart below illustrates the type of cumulative analysis that was possible in previous releases, where values evolved period after period rather than being displayed as isolated figures.

 

Since S/4HANA 2023, the application has been replaced by F6992 – Project Costs – Line Items.

Reference:

  • SAP Help document 

  • SAP Fiori App Library: F6992

However, the newer application appears to have lost several important analytical capabilities.

The cumulative measures mentioned above are no longer available, and commitment-related measures seem to have been removed as well. As a result:

  • Users can only analyze values for individual periods.

  • Cumulative cost evolution cannot be displayed.

  • Actual Cost + Commitments analysis is no longer possible.

  • Commitment visibility (open Purchase Requisitions and Purchase Orders) is missing.

  • Building project cost S-curves directly from the standard application is no longer feasible.

From a project controlling perspective, this feels like a regression in reporting capabilities rather than an enhancement.

Has anyone found an alternative standard application that provides the same cumulative and commitment-based analysis?

Is there any roadmap item, SAP Note, or Customer Influence request addressing the reintroduction of these measures in F6992?

I would be interested in understanding how other customers and consultants are handling this limitation in S/4HANA 2023 and 2025.

Regards
Marcio Blos

entry.title: I was unable to select the text from quick selection suggestions for the recently added entries
entry.content:
I was unable to select the text from quick selection suggestions for the recently added entries

  • Earlier i was able to select this with mouse selection but now mouse selection was disabled i need to click on arrow key and press enter

entry.title: Fiori screen - Custom Header Text ZH04 for Approver Field
entry.content: Requirement to show the approval header text in the Fiori screen, Initially its configured with Tasks in SWFVISU with INTENT here they have enhanced with custom field and it was shown, later the SAP ask to change the INTENT to INBOX_GEN - MY INBOX GENERIC APPLICATION to get some additional features, after that the custom field got disappeared now the user expects the custom field to show in Fiori screen which occurs in SAP traditional transaction VA03 of the header text of ZH04 for order perspective, How to get the same to satisfy the User and its around standard followed...Kindly share if you have come across any with the steps to follow. also attached screen details for your reference. Is there possible to add the field when it is INBOX_GEN? if its possible how do we go for solutioning

entry.title: Fiori Elements V2 (Adaptation Project) – Need to enforce mandatory field validation before Save/Appl
entry.content:
Hello SAP community,

I am working on a Fiori Elements V2 application (Object Page / List Report) using an Adaptation Project (SAP BAS – codeExt Controller Extensions + UI Adaptation).

I am trying to enforce a business requirement where a specific field must be mandatory before saving the document.

 

Technical Context

  • Application: Standard SAP Fiori Elements V2 app (Object Page)
  • Extension approach: Adaptation Project (no full custom controller, only Controller Extensions via codeExt)
  • Backend service: OData V2 service
  • Field source: Edm.Decimal field in OData entity
  • Field name: NoDaysReminder1
  • Annotation applied:
 
com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1.FieldControl = Mandatory
  • Field also appears inside a FieldGroup in Object Page sections.

 Observed Behavior

  • The field is displayed as mandatory in the UI (red asterisk appears correctly).
  • However, the application still allows saving the document when the field is empty.
  • No frontend validation blocks the Save/Apply action.

 What I already tried

1. Controller Extension (codeExt)

I attempted to override lifecycle hooks in the Object Page extension:

  • beforeSaveExtension
  • onAfterRendering
  • Custom validation logic

However, none of these hooks are triggered during Save/Apply in this Adaptation Project context.

2. UI interception attempts

I also tried:

  • Intercepting the Apply button (OverflowToolbar / DOM inspection)
  • Using event delegates on SmartField/Input controls
  • Using extensionAPI and TransactionController

None of these approaches allowed reliable interception of the Save event.

3. Metadata analysis

The OData metadata shows:

 

 
<Property Name="NoDaysReminder1"
          Type="Edm.Decimal"
          sap:field-control="NoDaysReminder1_fc" />

The field is NOT marked as Nullable="false", so from a service contract perspective it is optional.

Key Issue

It seems that:

  • FieldControl annotation only affects UI behavior
  • OData V2 service contract allows null values
  • Fiori Elements V2 does not expose a supported Save-interception hook in Adaptation Projects

Question to the community

Question to the community

Given the limitations of Fiori Elements V2 Adaptation Projects, what is the recommended SAP-supported pattern to enforce mandatory field validation BEFORE Save/Apply for a standard Object Page?

Specifically:

1. Is there any supported Fiori Elements V2 extension point (within Adaptation Projects / codeExt) that allows intercepting or validating the Save/Apply action?

2. If frontend-only enforcement is not supported, what is the recommended SAP backend approach for enforcing mandatory business validation in OData V2 services (e.g., BAdI, enhancement spots, gateway validations)?

3. In standard SAP design, should FieldControl be considered only a UI indication, or can it ever enforce transactional validation without backend support?

Additional context

This requirement is strictly frontend-driven at the moment, as backend modifications are currently uncertain or restricted.

Additional technical detail:

The application is part of SAP standard S/4HANA Fiori Elements V2 (Object Page) and is extended via SAP Business Application Studio Adaptation Project.

The extension is limited to controller extension artifacts generated via @SAP-ux/adp tooling (codeExt), meaning:

- No full override of standard controller lifecycle
- No access to Fiori Elements internal Save lifecycle hooks
- Limited access to extensionAPI hooks only

I am trying to confirm whether this is a limitation of the Adaptation Project framework or a misunderstanding of the available extension points in Fiori Elements V2.

Any clarification from SAP experts or official pattern references would be highly appreciated.

entry.title: Availability Control Consumes Budget Across G/L Accounts Within the Same Hierarchy
entry.content:
We want to implement Availability Control for Cost Centers in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. The customer only requires the control functionality, meaning they want the system to issue an error or warning message whenever the budget has been exceeded or is about to be exceeded.

However, we believe the current SAP behavior does not make sense. The system requires the combination of Cost Center – G/L Account to be included when uploading planning data using the "Import Financial Plan Data" app. Nevertheless, when a posting is made using a different G/L account that belongs to the same account hierarchy defined in the Availability Control configuration, the system still triggers the Availability Control message.

In other words, although the planning data is loaded at the specific Cost Center – G/L Account level, the Availability Control check appears to be performed at the hierarchy level configured in Availability Control, causing postings on other accounts within that hierarchy to consume the budget and trigger warnings or errors. We would like to understand whether this is the standard system behavior and the rationale behind this design.

entry.title: How to make specific fields read-only in an Action Parameter Popup (Abstract Entity) - ABAP 8.16
entry.content:
​I am working in an ABAP Release 8.16 environment using the RAP (ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model).

​I have a requirement where a custom action button on the UI triggers a popup dialog. This popup uses a custom Abstract Entity to define its input parameter fields.

​My Requirement:

When the user clicks the action button and the custom abstract entity popup appears, only the "Quantity" field should be editable. Every other field in that structure needs to be read-only.

​Here is a simplified version of my Abstract Entity:

@EndUserText.label: 'Action Parameter Popup'

define abstract entity ZC_MyActionParameter

{

  @EndUserText.label: 'Material ID'

  MaterialID : matnr;

  

  @EndUserText.label: 'Desired Quantity'

  Quantity : kwmeng;

  

  @EndUserText.label: 'Plant'

  Plant : werks_d;

}

 

And here is how it's defined in my Behavior Definition (BDEF):

action MyCustomAction parameter ZC_MyActionParameter result [1] $self;

Currently tried creating bdef for abstract entity and make feild readonly but not working 

And try to use object readonly UI annotation but is not available in this release

entry.title: Fiori Myinbox make a field clickable(hyperlink)
entry.content:
In fiori my inbox for PO Flexible workflow we have visualization parameter

Can we add a field and make it clickable (hyperlink) to open a different fiori app in new tab ?
Or Can we make an existing field clickable (hyperlink) to open a different fiori app in new tab ?

Please guide

entry.title: The title and subtitle for the applications created by Manage KPI and Reports application are empty
entry.content:
When an application created by Manage KPI and Reports is added as tile, application can be seen in Fiori but it has no title and subtitle even though title and subtitle is added in Manage KPI and Reports application. Problem occurs for both standart SAP apps like Supplier Evaluation by Quantity, Operational Supplier Evaluation and custom apps created by Manage KPI and Reports app.

A detailed document of the problem is added as attachment.

entry.title: Missing Auto-Fill Functionality on SAP TILES for all USERS
entry.content:
Missing Auto-Fill Functionality on SAP TILES for all USERS

Description: We notice a change on SAP behavior regarding the autofill functionality, 

Previously, it was possible to automatically populate certain fields, such, and others, by pressing the Space key. The system would suggest or retrieve recently used values, which significantly streamlined all process on SAP

This functionality appears to have disappeared. As a result, users are now required to manually enter this information each Time.

Could you please investigate whether this behavior is expected and has been intentionally changed? If not, could you check whether the previous Space key auto-fill functionality can be restored?

Example : On the Display Document Tile

Clicking on Space I have the list of previous document entered (the history) but I can’t choose by the mouse, I have to make the selection using the arrows on the keyboard

entry.title: Extensibility Options for Fiori App F4419 – Manage Requests for Long-Term Supplier’s Declarations
entry.content:
We are currently reviewing the extensibility options for SAP Fiori app F4419 – Manage Requests for Long-Term Supplier’s Declarations.

Technical details:

  • BSP Application: LLS_LTSDREQ_MNG
  • OData Service: LLS_LTSDREQUEST_MNG 
  • Application Component: FT_LEG_PRE_SDM

The SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library does not show any documented extension points for this app.

We have two requirements:

  1. We need to save additional custom information in a custom table when a supplier declaration request is created. Is there a supported BAdI, enhancement spot, business event, API, or backend extension point available during or after request creation?
  2. We need to control or limit the number of agreements included in a request, possibly based on the logged-in user or on a value selected by the user through a pop-up before creation. Is there a supported way to implement this through a backend validation or a Fiori elements/UI extension?
  3. If 1 and 2 are possible, are these done via SEGW or via ABAP RAP?

We are looking for an upgrade-safe solution and would prefer to avoid modifying SAP standard UI5, OData, or backend objects.

entry.title: Facing Issue in PageMap Extended Development
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Hi i have been developing Fiori Elements application. When we extend the application using PageMap, so i am setting the Show Restore On FB to true. 

Even after doing the changes and clearing the cache, i am not able to see the Reset Button in the Filter area. I tried to clear the cache and also preview the application again after doing the changes.

 


Can anyone in the community please help on this?
SAP Fiori

entry.title: F1861 - Display Supplier List : Change Responsive Table to Grid Table Using Adaptation Project.
entry.content:
Hi  Experts,

I am working on standard Sap Fiori app F1861 In S/4 Hana 2022 FPS02. Business wants to replace the responsive table with a grid table so user can a horizontal scrollbar and view all columns at ones. I tried changing the table type from all responsive to grid using an adaptation project, and also tried controller extension and manifest change, but the app fails at runtime with error 

"getTable().setFixedLayout is not a function",

and doesn't load properly. I also checked SAP KBA 3460918, and it says F1861 uses a responsive table by design, and no horizontal scroll is provided. Is there any officially supported way to change it to a grid table, maybe via CDS annotation or a sap note? Any guidance would be appreciated.

SAPUI5 SAP Fiori  SAP Fiori Elements

entry.title: Hierarchy tab greyed out at 'Display Financial Plan Data' fiori app
entry.content: After uploading data to fiori app 'Import Financial Plan Data', when we are checking the data via 'Display Financial Plan Data', expected results are coming but the 'Hierarchy Tab' is greyed out. Can someone say why it is greyed out?

entry.title: SAP Build Code BAS -/ui5-create-app fails with "@ui5/mcp-server" error and Accept Application button
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to create a freestyle SAPUI5 application in SAP Build Code (BAS) using the new Joule command:
/ui5-create-app

Prompt used

/ui5-create-app Create a freestyle SAPUI5 application for the Manage Sales Order CAP service.
Use the OData V4 service:
/service/manage_Sales_Order_App

CAP Project Information
I already have a CAP project generated successfully using Joule.
Project structure:
db/
srv/
app/
The OData service is available and generated correctly.

Joule Response
Joule returns the following proposal:
Let's configure your Manage Sales Order app using JavaScript instead of TypeScript.
Namespace: com.myorg.managesalesorder
Base Path:
/home/user/projects/Manage_Sales_Order_App/app
Create App Directory: true
OData V4 URL:
service/manage_Sales_Order_App/
OData Entity Set:
SalesOrders
TypeScript: false
Once you're ready, you can accept the application proposal
by pressing the "Accept Application" button.
Issue
The "Accept Application" button never appears.

At the same time, I can see the following error in the BAS Error panel:
Failed to generate app proposal:

Cannot find package '@ui5/mcp-server'
imported from

/extbin/local/openvscode-server/extensions/
sap-ux-help-extension-1.28.0/dist/extension-min.cjs

Did you mean to import
"@ui5/mcp-server/lib/api.js"?

Source:
SAP Fiori Tools - Guided Development

Troubleshooting Performed
I have already tried:

Refreshing BAS browser window
Opening a new Joule conversation
Using a simplified /ui5-create-app prompt
Creating a new Development Space
Reopening the project

The same error occurs every time.

Environment
SAP Build Code / SAP Business Application Studio
SAP Fiori Tools - Guided Development
Extension version reported in error:
sap-ux-help-extension-1.28.0
Questions
Is this a known issue with the SAP Fiori Tools / UI5 Create Application assistant?
Has anyone encountered the missing package error:
@ui5/mcp-server
Is there any workaround available until the issue is fixed?
Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Manage Service Entry Sheets App - Company Code, Plant and G/L Account columns not available
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and have observed a limitation in the Manage Service Entry Sheets app.

In the Settings (Table Personalization) of the Manage Service Entry Sheets app, the following fields are not available as selectable columns:

Company Code
Plant
G/L Account

Our customer operates across multiple Company Codes and Plants. Since these fields are not available in the list view, users are unable to easily identify, filter, or segregate Service Entry Sheets by Company Code or Plant.

Additionally, the G/L Account is only visible after opening an individual Service Entry Sheet and navigating to the line-item details. There is no option to display the G/L Account directly in the main list view.

In the customer's existing SAP ECC system, these fields are available in the list view, allowing users to review multiple Service Entry Sheets on a single screen without opening each document individually.

Steps to Reproduce

Open the Manage Service Entry Sheets app.
Navigate to the list of Service Entry Sheets.
Click Settings (Table Personalization).
Review the list of available columns.
Observe that Company Code, Plant, and G/L Account are not available as selectable columns.

Business Requirement

The customer processes a large volume of Service Entry Sheets across multiple Company Codes and Plants. Displaying these fields in the main list view would enable users to efficiently identify, filter, and monitor Service Entry Sheets without opening each document individually. The current behavior increases processing time and reduces operational efficiency.

Questions

Is this a standard limitation of the Manage Service Entry Sheets app in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
Is there any standard configuration, adaptation, or key user extensibility option available to display Company Code, Plant, and G/L Account in the list view?
If this is a standard limitation, is there any SAP roadmap item or recommended alternative to fulfill this business requirement?

I have attached screenshots showing the available columns in the Settings menu for reference.

Thank you in advance for your guidance.

entry.title: Fiori Cross Application Navigation to VL10C Opens Transaction Successfully but Does Not Prefill
entry.content:
A custom SAP Fiori application uses sap.ushell.services.CrossApplicationNavigation to launch VL10C/VL10F. Parameters VBELN (mapped to ST_VBELN-LOW), sap-ach=LE-SHP-GF, P_LERUL=2, and DYNP_OKCODE==S0S_TAB2 are passed through FLP target mapping.

The parameters are visible in the URL and navigation is successful; however, the Sales Order/Purchase Order tab is not selected by default, document fields are not populated, and the system displays "The entered selections were not applied".

  • Does SAP Fiori Launchpad intent-based navigation (Cross Application Navigation) support passing multiple values for parameter VBELN when launching WebGUI transactions such as VL10C and VL10F? If supported, what is the recommended approach for passing multiple document numbers?

  • If multiple values can be passed successfully, how can these values be automatically applied to the target transaction selection screen and populated in the corresponding fields (SD Document in VL10C and Purchasing Document in VL10F)?

  • Is there any specific configuration or supported mechanism to ensure that the Sales Orders tab in VL10C and the Purchase Orders tab in VL10F are automatically selected during launch, instead of opening on the default tab?
